items.summary: In a video uploaded to social media, Zubeyda Muzeyyen, known as DJ Haram, can be heard urging attendees to "oppose the Zio-Australian-Epstein empire" before she led the crowd with a chant.

items.title: Man faces court on murder charge after body found at Jung, Victoria
items.summary: A Victorian man appeared agitated as he faced court on a murder charge after a body was found in the small town of Jung earlier this week.

items.title: Labour Hire Authority links worker exploitation in the Yarra Valley to organised crime
items.summary: The Labour Hire Authority has accused an apple grower and labour-hire business of exploiting workers in the Yarra Valley, as concerns emerge about organised crime links.

items.title: Narelle is expected to become a category five cyclone — what does that mean?
items.summary: Cyclone Narelle is expected to intensify into a category five cyclone — find out what that means.

items.title: Liberals defend SA election flyer that confused Palestinian flag with another
items.summary: The South Australian Liberals have defended an election campaign flyer that attacks a Labor MP for his views on the Middle East and carries the slogan "Defend Australian Values", but appears to mistakenly show an image of a Sudanese flag instead of a Palestinian one.

items.title: Oil tankers have never stopped, industry assures, though they are in uncharted waters
items.summary: Ships carrying oil are continuing to come to Australia as expected and should do so for the next month, and the industry is urging that while the picture beyond that is uncertain and complicated, "the tankers have never stopped coming".

items.title: Tasmania to introduce planning law changes for 50 per cent larger granny flats
items.summary: Tasmanians will soon be able to build second dwellings that are 50 per cent larger than the current size. An urban planner says other jurisdictions should follow suit.

items.title: Body-in-suitcase double murderer Daniel Holdom appeals life sentences
items.summary: Daniel Holdom is appealing his life sentences for dumping the body of a toddler in a suitcase after he killed her and her mother.

items.title: Australian facilities damaged in Iranian attack on Middle East air base
items.summary: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has revealed that an Iranian projectile hit a road just outside the Al Minhad Air Base, but no Australians were injured.

items.title: BHP names Brandon Craig CEO as Woodside confirms Elizabeth Westcott as leader
items.summary: BHP has tapped Brandon Craig as its new CEO, while Elizabeth Westcott has taken the top job at Woodside Energy three months after she served as acting CEO.

items.title: Is antibacterial or regular hand soap best for killing germs?
items.summary: Experts say there is an array of options when it comes to hand soaps, and some labels can be misleading.

items.title: Fuel costs from Iran war forcing Australians to cancel domestic road travel, flights, cruises
items.summary: Domestic travel is also taking a hit as a result of rising fuel costs across the globe, with regional communities likely to feel the most impact.

items.title: Joe Kent joins growing list of Donald Trump allies distancing themselves from Iran war
items.summary: After years of threats, insults and throwing the global world order into chaos, Donald Trump is being reminded at home and abroad that trust isn't just a nicety, it's essential when you need allies.

items.title: Aboriginal rangers meet on NSW South Coast to merge past and present
items.summary: The first Aboriginal rangers conference for the NSW South Coast focused on how to best take care of country by combining cultural knowledge from elders with new technology.

items.title: Bank of America reaches tentative settlement over Epstein-linked transactions lawsuit
items.summary: Bank of America has tentatively settled a lawsuit claiming it ignored suspicious financial transactions involving Jeffrey Epstein while he was sexually abusing hundreds of girls and women.

items.title: Man accused of woman and baby's murders suffers from schizoaffective disorder, court hears
items.summary: Blake Seers has been charged with two counts of murder after the bodies of a 37-year-old woman and an 11-month-old girl were found inside a home at Belivah, south of Brisbane, last week.

items.title: Police charge Qld Young Australian of the Year with possessing child exploitation material
items.summary: Police allege illegal material was found on the youth support officer's work and personal devices. He has received several awards for his work with young people in the town of Cunnamulla after he founded a neighbourhood centre.

items.title: John Travolta's old luxury jet bound for Australian aviation museum by ship
items.summary: The long-awaited fuselage and wings of John Travolta's Boeing 707, which he is donating to a regional NSW aircraft museum, have been loaded onto a ship in the US, bound for Australia.

items.title: Silverton gardener's 'little oasis' featured in Gardening Australia magazine
items.summary: At Helen Murray's home in Silverton in far west New South Wales, where temperatures often push past 30 degrees Celsius in summer, figuring out what will grow is often a jigsaw puzzle of trial and error.

items.title: Denmark's King Frederik and Queen Mary spend second day in Melbourne
items.summary: After a whirlwind of engagements yesterday, Danish King Frederik and Queen Mary open a renewable energy conference, meet with stem cell researchers and highlight youth mental health in Melbourne.

items.title: Farmers and conservationists form 'emergency alliance' against invasive species

items.title: Fuel supply chain coordinator to be proposed at national cabinet meeting
items.summary: The prime minister will convene a virtual gathering of state and territory leaders tomorrow, with the talks to focus on strengthening state-federal cooperation in the face of demand-driven shortages on fuel.

items.title: Federal government suffers High Court loss over legality of curfews and ankle bracelets
items.summary: New government measures to monitor people released from indefinite immigration detention are struck down by the High Court in Canberra.

items.title: Forget fireworks, Bali's new year is marked with silence, fasting, meditation
items.summary: Instead of fireworks and countdowns, Bali rings in its new year in complete silence.

items.title: Senegal stripped of AFCON title, Morocco declared winner months after controversial final
items.summary: Senegal's 1-0 win in the Africa Cup of Nations final has been overturned by an appeals board and a 3-0 victory awarded to Morocco after Senegalese players left the pitch in protest during the controversial January clash.

items.summary: Detectives praise the bravery of more than a dozen women who testified against Bunbury massage therapist and rapist Anthony Brain, including Anwen Handmer.

items.title: Adelaide 36ers into NBL grand final after Bryce Cotton masterclass
items.summary: To chants of "MVP", Bryce Cotton scored 38 points to steer the Adelaide 36ers past South East Melbourne and into their first grand final since 2018.

items.title: Matildas coach Joe Montemurro explains Hayley Raso absence in Asian Cup semifinal win over China

items.title: Who is Anna Lapwood, the content creator bringing organ music to millions on social media
items.summary: Anna Lapwood uses social media to share her love of the organ and favourite music from Bach to Lord of the Rings. Millions follow her and thousands more come to her concerts.

items.summary: The Bureau of Meteorology is set to issue a cyclone watch alert for parts of the Northern Territory from tomorrow, as residents attempting to clean up after devastating floods brace for more rain.

items.title: Kyle Sandilands says ARN Media has terminated his $100m radio contract
items.summary: Kyle Sandilands has announced he has been sacked by ARN Media after an on-air row with co-host Jackie Henderson last month.

items.summary: Farmers are warning consumers to expect price rises in the supermarket as fuel and fertiliser shortages affect crop production and transport.

items.title: Multiple demolition derbies cancelled after SafeWork NSW rules barriers unsafe
items.summary: Agriculture show societies say late, inconsistent rulings by SafeWork NSW about safety precautions for demolition derbies have cost them thousands in attendance and ticket sales.

items.title: One Nation is tipped to be a major player in the South Australian election

items.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Matildas' butterfly effect on display in win over China, but their evolution isn't complete

items.title: Iran's communications blackout leaves family in Australia in the dark
items.summary: Iranian Australians with family in Iran are concerned for their loved ones, with internet and phone blackouts making it near-impossible to stay in contact.

items.title: Iran confirms intelligence minister killed in Israeli strike, 12 dead in Beirut attack
items.summary: Tehran confirms that an Israeli attack killed Iran's intelligence minister, Esmaeil Khatib, in the latest high profile attack on the regime's leadership. Read how the day unfolded.

items.title: Petrol price spiral could put mobile early childcare education services at risk

items.title: Medical advocacy is crucial for young women but it can feel like a 'catch-22'
items.summary: Young women have detailed their experiences being dismissed and misdiagnosed as chronic pain sufferers. Now, they're teaching others how to advocate for their health.

items.title: In war-weary Lebanon, anger at Israel and Hezbollah takes hold
items.summary: As Israel's intense bombardment continues into a third week, Lebanon's war-weary population and cash-strapped government are wondering when the fighting will stop.

items.title: Cyclone Narelle could be the first storm in 21 years to make landfall three times
items.summary: The last Australian triple landfall system was Severe Tropical Cyclone Ingrid more than two decades ago.

items.title: CSIRO survey finds drop in community support for Tasmanian salmon industry
items.summary: A CSIRO report has found Tasmania's salmon farming industry has experienced a "significant and sustained shift" away from strong community support it experienced a decade ago.

items.title: Cancer nurse banned for stealing medication from Royal Adelaide Hospital
items.summary: A registered nurse has been banned for stealing opioid medication while working on an oncology ward at the Royal Adelaide Hospital, but a tribunal has allowed her to return to practise with strict conditions.

items.title: Court finds Japanese sterilisation law 'lacks rationality' and should be debated
items.summary: A law in Japan bans childless women from accessing voluntary sterilisation. Now a Tokyo judge has found it "lacks rationality" and should be debated.

items.title: VIQ Solutions Australia placed into voluntary administration, court transcriptions services in limbo
items.summary: Four weeks after the ABC revealed sensitive court files were being accessed offshore in a potential risk to national security, the company responsible for the breach has entered into voluntary administration.

items.title: Apartment height limits locked in across 25 Melbourne activity centres

items.title: Labor hasn't got a capital gains tax policy yet, but it's already making the case
items.summary: A Senate committee report into the capital gains tax was authored by the Greens but littered with clues about Labor's plans.

items.title: Reserve Bank governor warns of the recession we might have to have after interest rate hike
items.summary: Is the RBA fighting last year's inflation war with its latest interest rate hike? Whatever the case may be, Australians are bracing for even more economic pain.

items.title: Middle East war forces pharmaceutical companies to reroute critical medicines to ensure Australian supply
items.summary: The Middle East war is forcing major pharmaceutical companies to divert critical medicines away from one of the world's most important trade routes, to ensure there are no supply issues in Australia.

items.title: Behind Melbourne Museum's shiny facade sits a world hidden from view
items.summary: Museums display only a fraction of their collection so we went behind the scenes at the Melbourne Museum to find the untold stories kept from public view.

items.title: Do Not Pass Go, a new dark comedy from Melbourne Theatre Company, might inspire you to quit your job
items.summary: This dark comedy takes aim at capitalism and corporate work culture, but it also finds hope in the relationships between everyday people.

items.title: Travel quiz, March 18: Do you have the frequent flyers points to pass our test?
items.summary: Which country’s currency is called the quetzal? And what is Thailand's Songkran festival best known for?

items.summary: Sam Kerr gets her angles right, Katrina Gorry stands tall despite a battering and Kaitlyn Torpey has a birthday to remember. Here are the quick hits from the Matildas' exhilirating 2-1 semifinal triumph over China.

items.summary: When Israeli and US strikes killed Ayatollah Ali Khamenei at the start of the Middle East war, Iran's security chief Ali Larijani became even more powerful than he had been for decades.

items.title: Sri Lanka introduces four-day work week to conserve fuel amid Middle East war
items.summary: Sri Lanka has introduced a shorter work week for people to use less fuel amid the ongoing Middle East war.

items.title: Israel moves troops into Lebanon

items.title: Israel's invasion of Lebanon is pushing a fragile nation to its 'tipping point'
items.summary: Israel is moving more troops into southern Lebanon ahead of what some fear will be a long occupation, and experts warn further destabilisation could threaten the country's existence.

items.title: Trump's Venezuelan Oil Grab: What happens now?
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Men accused of killing Canberra grandmother Irma Palasics 'went there for money, not murder', jury hears
items.summary: A jury sees a covert police recording of Melbourne man Steve Fabriczy admitting to being involved in the burglary where Canberra grandmother Irma Palasics was killed in 1999, as he faces trial for her murder.

items.title: Defence minister confirms US can access data collected by Australian surveillance plane in Gulf
items.summary: The defence minister has confirmed intelligence gathered by an Australian surveillance aircraft deployed to support Gulf states can be accessed by the United States, potentially informing its strikes on Iran.

items.title: Childcare worker Arvind Ajay Singh on trial for alleged rape of child
items.summary: A childcare worker is standing trial over allegations he raped a young child on Queensland's Sunshine Coast.
